titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5956

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=784 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.781324+00 | translation moa=785 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.792513+00

p.cl: . Omnibus Christi fidelibus hoc scriptum visuris uel Audituris. Bertramus de Ristona salutem.

me Concessisse. et hac presenti carta mea Confirmasse vendicionem. quietam clamacionem.
et abiuractionem omnium terrarum quam Ricardus filius Henrici Cisoris de Dunelm’ vendidit domino
Ricardo Priori et Conuentui de Coldingham in villa et in territorio parue Ristone cum omnibus pertinent
iis et exactis infra villam et extra sine aliquo Retinemento sicut planius et plenius continetur
in carta confecta Quam dicti Prior et Conuentus habent de prefato Ricardo Habendam et Tenen
dam sepedictis priori et Conuentui de Coldingham et eorum successoribus in perptuum libere. quiete. et honori
ffice in omnibus faciendo omne forinsecum seruicium pertinens ad dominum Regem et ad dominum de Col
dingham quantum pertinet ad dimidiam carucatam terre in eadem villa. Reddendo inde annuatim mihi
et heredibus meis duos solidos tantum pro omnibus que de dicta terra exigi poterunt. ad duos term
inos anni. silicet duodecim denarios ad festum sancti Iohannis Baptiste. et duodecim denarios ad festum
sancti Martini in hyeme.
To all Christ's faithful who shall view or hear this writing, Bertram of Reston sends greeting.

to have granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm, the sale, quitclaim, and abjuration of all the lands which Richard son of Henry Scissor of Durham sold to the lord Richard the Prior, and the Convent of Coldingham, in the toun and in the territory of Little Reston, with all pertinents and exactions inside the toun and outside, without any withholding, just as it is plainly and fully contained in the charter drawn up, which the said Prior and Convent had from the aforementioned Richard. For the oft-mentioned Prior and Convent of Coldingham, and their successors, to have and to hold for ever; freely, quitely, and honourably in all things; performing all forinsec service pertaining to the lord King and to the lord of Coldingham, as much as pertains to half a ploughgate of land in the same toun; rendering thereupon to me and my heirs annually two shillings only in return for all things which can be exacted from the said land; at two terms of the year, that is, twelve pence at the feast of Saint John the Baptist, and twelve pence at the feast of Saint Martin in the winter.
